heading: ## Fabro runtime constraints
reason: Operator-procedure constraint over an EXTERNAL runtime the plugin does not own (the Fabro engine binary): it governs which Fabro build the factory may pin - the `factory-integration` carrier branch, its composition, the < 0.256 base ceiling, and the rebuild/re-pin duty - none of which is a code path the plugin executes, so there is no plugin-side behavior for a test to exercise. Per the amended file preamble, the deciding command is `fabro --version` and enforcement is review-time verification against it. A real test ID becomes bindable if the ceiling is later enforced by a dispatch-preflight engine-version gate (ledger bd-ib-j9x), which WOULD be plugin-observable behavior and would carry its own scenario.

heading: ## Scenario 7 — Regroom an oversized work-item
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the groom backlog-decomposition front-end's mechanical seam (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.commands.groom): the Scenario-7 journey (draft read-only via load_groom_context -> on approval file_approved_slices files approved factory slices through intake routing with dependency edges linked, routes the spec-change slice to /livespec:propose-change, and explicitly closes the original backlog item as regroomed-out) is exercised through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend). The bound node id is the end-to-end approval journey; read-only-until-approval, the dependency-edge linkage, the all-spec-change refuse-don't-drop guarantee, and the non-backlog-target / unknown-id expected errors are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 8 — Intake Definition-of-Ready triage
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the shared intake Definition-of-Ready checklist (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.intake_dor) that all three capture front-ends call: the three Scenario-8 cases (single-acceptance->ready, epic->needs-regroom, non-verifiable-or-blocked->not-yet-actionable) are exercised through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend). The bound node id is the single-acceptance->ready journey; the epic->needs-regroom and non-verifiable/blocked->not-yet-actionable cases are sibling tests in the same module, with the per-gate precedence pinned by evaluate().

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the needs-regroom state machine (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.regroom): the three Scenario-9 transitions are exercised through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend). The bound node id is the exit-by-filing-ready-slices journey; the entry-via-DoR-failure and entry-via-non-convergence-bounce transitions are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 10 — Dispatcher never auto-approves a manual-admission spec-change item

clauses.scenario: Scenario 10 — Dispatcher never auto-approves a manual-admission spec-change item

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 10 under the ratified approval model: a spec-change / risky item rests at pending-approval when its effective admission_policy is manual, is surfaced for the maintainer, and is never launched into a Fabro sandbox. The dispatch CLI is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient with run_dispatch recorded; sibling cases in the same module cover journaling and the auto-policy path.

heading: ## Scenario 11 — Dispatcher bounces a non-converging slice to backlog

clauses.scenario: Scenario 11 — Dispatcher bounces a non-converging slice to backlog

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the Dispatcher's non-convergence bounce (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei, gap-rs4tkntz): under the work-item-state-machine lifecycle the bounce target is the first-class backlog status (the slice leaves the WIP for re-grooming), not the prior needs-regroom label. The dispatch CLI is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend); the bound node id is the stalled-slice bounce-to-backlog journey; the DOT-marker bounce, the no-bounce-on-ordinary-failure/green cases, and the predicate cases are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 12 — Dispatcher emits calibration telemetry
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the Dispatcher's calibration telemetry (work-item livespec-impl-beads-yfsv4j, gap-ajq7ynr4): the dispatch CLI is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend) to a terminal outcome, then the EXISTING on-disk Dispatcher journal (tmp/fabro-dispatch-journal.jsonl) is read back, proving a single `calibration` stage record carries the full outcome signal plus the mechanical size proxies. The bound node id is the outcome-and-size-proxies journey; the unobservable-proxy-is-null (not zero) case, the rides-the-existing-journal-with-no-new-service case, the non-convergence bounced_to_regroom case, and the journal-record-shape pin are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 13 — Calibration analysis pass proposes advisory thresholds

clauses.scenario: Scenario 13 — Calibration analysis pass proposes advisory thresholds

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the periodic calibration analysis pass (livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro.calibration_analysis): a real on-disk Dispatcher journal seeded with behavior 6's flat calibration-stage records is read back through the production seam and analyze_calibration proposes ceiling thresholds. The bound node id is the advisory-and-never-auto-enforced guardrail (the proposal flags advisory/not-adopted and mutates no journal state); the correlation-proposes-a-ceiling Then, the not-an-always-on-service Then, the decline-on-no-signal cases, and the no-false-zero proxy handling are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Scenario 14 — Fabro non-convergence routes back to the Dispatcher

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the single Fabro workflow-DOT tweak (work-item livespec-impl-beads-rw75ym, gap-f7kcvboh): the shipped .fabro/workflows/implement-work-item/workflow.fabro is read and asserted to carry the fix-loop cap (the existing janitor node-visit governor) plus a non-converged exit edge (janitor Exhausted -> the non_converged terminal command node, NOT the human gate) that routes back to the Dispatcher, within Fabro's existing DOT vocabulary and requiring no platform/setup change. The bound node id is the exit-edge-routing assertion; the cap, terminal-node, and shared-sentinel cases are sibling tests in the same module.

heading: ## Closed-item integrity
reason: Added by the closed-item-integrity revise pass (v007). The shared heading_coverage check is satisfied by enumerating every SPECIFICATION/ H2 heading; the closed-item-integrity invariant's mechanical guard (the closed_item_integrity check) is not yet built, so this constraint heading binds to its real test via the governed propose-change/revise resulting_files[] loop once the gap-tied impl work-item that wires the check lands its exercising test. The clauses[] gap-id->scenario link is deferred to that implementation step (impl-beads ships no clauses[] entries or livespec_spec_clauses extractor yet; adopting the clauses[] map is itself the first implementation work-item).

reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the closed_item_integrity dev-tooling check (work-items livespec-impl-beads-fxt/xe0/oaq; gap-j2femmn7 invariant, gap-gtamowt3 + gap-3wbdenoy check): the check's main() is driven through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient (the hermetic CI backend) with a fixture tests/heading-coverage.json carrying the clauses[] gap-id->scenario map. The bound node id is the case-1 finding journey (a TODO-bound closed gap-tied item surfaces a closed-item-integrity warning at exit 0 in the default warn mode); the fail-mode exit-non-zero, the missing-resolution:completed-label, the unresolvable-gap-id, and the case-2 fully-proven-no-finding cases are sibling tests in the same module. Bound at the gap-tied impl step before its own resolution:completed closure (the check is its own first subject).

heading: ## Scenario 17 — drive operator-surface defaults
reason: Rebind pending the drive rename rename impl follow-up (proposal drive-rename-and-next-asymmetry, Change F/J): Scenario 17 was re-scoped to drive and its bare-drive-walkthrough sub-scenario RETIRED, so the prior bound test (retired bare-drive walkthrough test) no longer describes a live scenario. The real integration-tier node id for the surviving cwd-default + Markdown-default drive defaults is populated when the drive-rename impl follow-up binds it via the governed resulting_files[] loop.

heading: ## Worker credential projection
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the worker credential-projection contract (work-items bd-ib-di442r, bd-ib-3ti4jf, bd-ib-g7e34u): the bound node id drives the Dispatcher overlay materialization seam with injected host credential, clock, and fleet manifest, then proves the run-scoped mode-600 overlay carries both the existing Claude OAuth env and the Codex auth.json file projection materialized through a prepare step. Sibling tests in tests/integration/test_worker_credential_projection_scenarios18_19.py and tests/livespec_orchestrator_beads_fabro/commands/test_dispatcher_dual_cred.py cover stale-credential refusal, direct host auth.json read, non-rotatable snapshot projection, and Codex implementer adapter selection. The live dual-provider dispatch proof remains a provisioned-environment acceptance check because this host lacks the required Fabro LLM provider and sandbox provisioning.

heading: ## Scenario 18 — Dispatcher projects a non-rotatable subscription credential into a worker sandbox
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 18: the bound node id exercises the Dispatcher materialization seam and verifies a fresh host Codex credential is transformed into a non-rotatable snapshot, carried in the overlay, and written by a sandbox prepare step to `$CODEX_HOME/auth.json` while the Claude OAuth env remains present in the same overlay. Lower-level sibling tests prove the refresh token is replaced with the inert sentinel and never leaks the host refresh token. The scenario's worker-authenticates clause still needs the first live dual-provider dispatch proof in a Fabro-provisioned environment.

heading: ## Scenario 19 — Dispatcher refuses dispatch when the credential freshness gate fails
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 19: the bound node id drives the Dispatcher materialization seam with an injected stale host credential and proves dispatch refuses before writing the run-scoped overlay, surfacing the `codex login` renewal message instead of projecting a credential that cannot outlive the run budget. Sibling tests cover the missing-credential refusal and the pure freshness-gate boundary.

heading: ## Scenario 20 — Review gate routes a green build through code review before PR
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 20: the bound node id inspects the shipped implement-work-item Fabro graph and verifies the implementer nodes (`implement`, `fix`, `pr`, and `review_fix`) use the Codex-routable `acp_adapter` input while the review-only node uses the separate Claude `review_adapter`; sibling tests prove the review adapter is pinned through ANTHROPIC_MODEL + CLAUDE_CODE_EFFORT_LEVEL and no ACP node hard-codes the Claude adapter. The gate is BLOCKING by default: a past-cap blocking finding escalates the item to blocked / needs-human unless the effective merge_on_review_cap ships it (contracts.md §"Dispatcher policy settings"); the graph's approve/fix edge behavior is codified in the workflow DOT, and the blocking/ship-on-cap disposition binds its test when the dispatcher-policy-settings implementation slice lands.

heading: ## Scenario 21 — Codex plugin discovery and full-access posture
reason: Top-of-pyramid live Codex TUI acceptance for the operator discovery path: starts the real Codex TUI, opens /skills, chooses List skills, searches the short name drive, and requires the rendered Skill row to include the livespec-orchestrator-beads-fabro plugin context. The merged scenario also carries the manifest-gated full-access posture sub-scenarios (fleet-listed ON, unlisted default-off, gate-unevaluable fail-closed); those are covered by unit tests under tests/hooks and tests/hooks_plugin but are NOT bound to this e2e picker test, which still covers only the discovery half.

heading: ## Work-item state semantics
reason: Ratified at the approval-model restoration (design record: repo thewoolleyman/livespec, plan/archive/work-item-state-machine, decisions 26/32): plain-English semantics for the seven lifecycle states, the approve-is-pending-approval-to-ready transition rule, the maintainer's verbatim rationale, and the intent-preservation clauses. Definitional semantics + process clauses; the transition behaviors are exercised via Scenarios 8/10/23/31/33's bindings, and the post-ratification rework slice binds the re-targeted behaviors when its tests land.

heading: ## Dispatcher admission, WIP cap, and post-merge acceptance
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the approval/admission mechanics, post-merge acceptance, and the per-repo WIP cap. The bound node id drives the loop through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient: pending auto-policy items are approved into ready and admitted up to wip_cap with assignee set, while over-cap work waits at ready; Scenario 23 covers the manual pending-approval hold, Scenario 24 covers complete -> acceptance, and Scenario 25 covers acceptance_policy plus reject routing. An item's effective admission_policy / acceptance_policy is its own label when it carries one, otherwise the global dispatcher.auto_approve_ready / dispatcher.acceptance_mode default (contracts.md §"Dispatcher policy settings"); the failing-AI-pass rework route is covered by Scenario 35.

heading: ## Scenario 22 — Dispatcher admits the top-ranked ready item up to the per-repo WIP cap
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for the admission valve + per-repo WIP cap (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): with wip_cap=2 and three admission-eligible ready items ranked a0/a1/a2, the loop admits a0 and a1 (sets an assignee, transitions each to active) and leaves a2 at ready (capacity-deferred). Driven through the real dispatcher loop CLI + store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient with run_dispatch recorded.

heading: ## Scenario 23 — Dispatcher never auto-approves a manual-admission item
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 23 under the ratified approval model: an effective-manual item at pending-approval is held there, surfaced on every Dispatcher pass for explicit approve, and never auto-approved into ready or launched. The test exercises dispatcher.main loop mode through the real store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient.

heading: ## Scenario 24 — complete merges on green into the acceptance state
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for complete merges-on-green into acceptance (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): a green dispatch transitions the active item to the observable acceptance state (merged + live) rather than straight to done. Driven through the real dispatch CLI + store/client seam against the in-memory FakeBeadsClient; the journal shows ledger-complete + acceptance-ai-pass and no ledger-accept under the default ai-then-human policy.

heading: ## Scenario 25 — accept confirms post-ship per acceptance_policy
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for accept-per-acceptance_policy (slice bd-ib-dnw2ei): ai-then-human parks the merged item in acceptance until a human confirms; the bound node id is the ai-then-human park journey. The effective acceptance_policy is the item's own label, or the global dispatcher.acceptance_mode default when it carries none, and the AI pass is a read-and-judge of the merged diff plus a telemetry watch yielding PASS or FAIL (contracts.md §"Dispatcher policy settings"). The ai-only-confirms-to-done case and the reject routing (rework -> active; re-groom -> backlog, exercised through the real store seam + the pure reject_routing) are sibling tests in the same module; the FAIL routes are covered by Scenario 35.

heading: ## Scenario 31 — drive human valve actions
reason: Integration-tier acceptance for Scenario 31 through drive.run_action and the real FakeBeadsClient-backed store seam: approve:<id> performs pending-approval -> ready and records the operator journal. Sibling tests in the same module cover set-admission and set-acceptance policy edits as label-only writes that leave status unchanged, including the manual -> auto pending-approval no-surprise-transition case.

heading: ## Dispatcher policy settings
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: the spec-altitude statement of the independent dispatcher.* policy settings (each a global default, each except the per-repo wip_cap overridable per work-item by a ledger label that WINS over the global), settable via the orchestrator API and the console, with every safety floor preserved. Integration-tier test id populated as the clause->scenario->test binding lands; the behaviors are exercised by Scenarios 33-37.

heading: ## Dispatcher policy settings
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: the wire surface of the three policy settings (auto_approve_ready, acceptance_mode, merge_on_review_cap), the two rework caps (review_fix_cap, acceptance_rework_cap), the no-per-item-override wip_cap, the always-escalate-needs-human floor, the per-disposition audit journal, and the API-configurable completeness principle. Integration-tier test id populated as the clause->scenario->test binding lands; the behaviors are exercised by Scenarios 33-37.

heading: ## Dispatcher policy settings constraints
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: the mechanically-checkable safety rails (safe defaults; audit every auto-disposition; still escalate the truly-unresolvable and hold the no-release-with-zero-verification floor; console Settings / inline help / settings-doc completeness). Integration-tier test id populated as the clause->scenario->test binding lands; Scenario 37 binds the safe-defaults rail.

heading: ## Scenario 33 — auto_approve_ready governs admission and a per-item label wins
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: an unlabeled routine item inherits the global dispatcher.auto_approve_ready and is auto-approved into ready; an explicit per-item admission_policy: manual label BEATS the permissive global and rests the item at pending-approval; a design-human-gated (spec-change-tier) item is never auto-approved under any setting or label. Integration-tier test id populated when the implementing slice binds it.

heading: ## Scenario 34 — acceptance_mode governs the acceptance leg
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: dispatcher.acceptance_mode selects the acceptance leg (ai-only accepts to done on a passing AI pass; ai-then-human parks for the human accept valve; human-only parks with the AI pass advisory), a per-item acceptance_policy label overrides the global, and every path carries at least one AI pass. Integration-tier test id populated when the implementing slice binds it.

heading: ## Scenario 35 — A failing AI acceptance pass reworks only in the AI-dispositive modes
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: a FAILING AI acceptance pass auto-reworks the item to active ONLY when the effective acceptance_policy is ai-only or ai-then-human, bounded by dispatcher.acceptance_rework_cap (past the cap it escalates to blocked / needs-human); under human-only the failing pass is ADVISORY - the item stays parked and the human keeps the accept / reject decision. Integration-tier test id populated when the implementing slice binds it.

heading: ## Scenario 36 — Every needs-human block always escalates
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: no policy setting auto-disposes a blocked_reason: needs-human item or a design-human-gated decision (drift acceptance, spec-change slice, regroom / backlog bounce, human-only acceptance) - each is surfaced to a human even at high confidence, and the escalation is queryable from the journal. Integration-tier test id populated when the implementing slice binds it.

heading: ## Scenario 37 — Safe defaults hold when nothing is configured
reason: Dispatcher-policy-settings revise: with no dispatcher.* policy settings configured and no per-item policy label, auto_approve_ready and merge_on_review_cap are false, acceptance_mode is ai-then-human, review_fix_cap is 3 and acceptance_rework_cap is 2 - and no UNLABELED item is auto-approved, no past-cap review ships, and no acceptance reaches done without a human (the constraints.md safe-defaults rail; scoped to unlabeled items because a per-item label legitimately beats a safe global). Integration-tier test id populated when the implementing slice binds it.

heading: ## Scenario 46 — Per-item cap overrides set a label or clear to reinherit the global default
reason: TODO: bind to an INTEGRATION-TIER-or-above test (never a unit-tier test — a scenario describes user-observable behavior, per `SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy"). Added by the enumerate-per-item-override-and-move-drive-actions revise: the three per-item cap-override drive verbs (set-merge-on-review-cap boolean, set-review-fix-cap / set-acceptance-rework-cap positive-int) write a per-item cap label without touching status, and the reserved `clear` value removes the label so the item reinherits the global dispatcher.* default, with clear-when-absent a green no-op. The exercising test binds through drive.run_action and the FakeBeadsClient-backed store seam (b620304 + the clear-to-inherit commit).

heading: ## Scenario 47 — The guarded move relocates within the operator-movable statuses only
reason: TODO: bind to an INTEGRATION-TIER-or-above test (never a unit-tier test — a scenario describes user-observable behavior, per `SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Heading taxonomy"). Added by the enumerate-per-item-override-and-move-drive-actions revise: the guarded move drive action writes ONLY the item's status among the operator-movable set {backlog, ready, blocked, active} and refuses done / acceptance / pending-approval with a clear error, preserving the accept-from-acceptance ship-guard. The exercising test binds through drive.run_action and the FakeBeadsClient-backed store seam (b620304).

The same module carries the paired negative case proving `.github/actions/` composite-action edits are admitted.

heading: ## Codex full-access runtime constraints
reason: External-runtime posture constraint over Codex and the OpenAI Codex companion plugin: the deciding signals are the local opt-in/marker gate, the canary's exact-expression classification of the active companion source, the fail-closed-on-authorization default, and the rendered raw codex exec full-access/EOF behavior. Unit coverage for the gate and canary exists under tests/hooks and tests/hooks_plugin; a single bindable test ID for the whole constraint awaits an integration-tier test that exercises the gate and canary end to end.
